title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. calcicarpetanus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. calcicarpetanus, also known as the Welsh Daffodil, is a subspecies of wild daffodil that is native to limestone pavement in Wales. This plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to possess medicinal properties beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. calcicarpetanus include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the flowers in water or oil, and have been used in traditional folk medicine for treating a range of ailments.
